challenge: Designing a scalable user tracking system that can handle habit streaks, progress metrics, and AI-generated recommendations without overcomplicating the data model.
solution: Implemented PostgreSQL-based progress tracking with a normalized schema. Used materialized views for analytics dashboards to avoid expensive real-time queries.
lessonLearned: Premature optimization created unnecessary complexity. Starting with the simplest working schema and iterating based on actual usage patterns proved far more effective.
nextStep: Build the AI habit recommendation engine using user behavioral patterns.

challenge: Setting up authentication and protected routes with session management that works across the Next.js frontend and FastAPI backend.
solution: Implemented NextAuth for the frontend with JWT tokens passed to the FastAPI backend. Created middleware for route protection and session validation.
lessonLearned: Cross-framework auth is more nuanced than single-framework solutions. Token refresh logic needs careful handling to avoid silent session drops.
nextStep: Design the user onboarding flow and initial goal-setting experience.
